Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The city council voted on Tuesday to approve a new bike lane network spanning twelve kilometres, with construction expected to begin in spring and finish before next year's transit summit.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: Researchers at the university published a study showing that the local bee population declined by eighteen percent over the past five years, largely due to habitat loss and pesticide use.
Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The airline announced it will add three new direct routes to South America starting next quarter, citing rising demand from business travellers.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A local bakery chain is expanding into two neighbouring towns after securing a small business loan, with the first new location expected to open by the end of the year.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The transit authority confirmed that the downtown subway extension will open two months ahead of schedule, following faster-than-expected tunnel construction.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A regional hospital network announced a partnership with a telehealth startup to provide free virtual consultations to rural patients starting next spring.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The national park service reported that wolf reintroduction efforts have led to a marked recovery in local elk and vegetation populations over the past decade.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A mid-sized furniture manufacturer is shifting its entire production line to reclaimed wood, citing both cost savings and demand from environmentally conscious buyers.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: City officials unveiled a plan to convert an abandoned rail line into a fifteen-kilometre pedestrian and cycling greenway, with the first section opening next summer.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: Researchers found that a newly discovered species of deep-sea coral can survive water temperatures previously thought lethal to most reef-building organisms.
Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The school district approved funding for a free breakfast program in all elementary schools after a pilot showed improved attendance and test scores.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A logistics company is testing electric delivery bikes in three congested city centers, reporting a thirty percent drop in average delivery times so far.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: The museum announced it will digitize its entire photo archive over the next three years, making over two hundred thousand historical images publicly searchable online.

Summarize the following in exactly one sentence: A community farming cooperative doubled its harvest yield this year after switching to a crop rotation system recommended by a local agricultural extension office.

Three friends, Sam, Jo, and Lee, each own a different pet: cat, dog, bird. Sam does not own the bird. Jo owns the dog. Who owns the cat?
Three friends, Ivy, Noah, and Mia, each drive a different colored car: red, blue, green. Ivy's car isn't red. Noah's car is green. Who drives the blue car?
Four friends, Kai, Lena, Theo, and Ren, each drink a different beverage: tea, coffee, juice, water. Kai doesn't drink tea or water. Lena drinks coffee. Theo doesn't drink water. Who drinks water?
Three musicians, Nora, Pablo, and Yuki, each play a different instrument: piano, violin, drums. Nora doesn't play drums. Pablo plays violin. Who plays the drums?
Three friends, Grace, Hugo, and Elin, each pick a different fruit: mango, kiwi, fig. Grace didn't pick the fig. Hugo picked kiwi. Who picked the fig?
Four friends, Owen, Priya, Sana, and Tariq, each play a different sport: tennis, chess, rugby, golf. Owen doesn't play chess or golf. Priya plays rugby. Sana doesn't play golf. Who plays golf?
Three friends, Zara, Finn, and Talia, each drink a different beverage: tea, soda, juice. Zara doesn't drink soda. Finn drinks juice. Who drinks tea?
Three friends, Omar, Bea, and Nils, each play a different game: chess, checkers, dominoes. Omar doesn't play dominoes. Bea plays checkers. Who plays dominoes?
Three friends, Petra, Junho, and Alexei, each prefer a different activity: hiking, swimming, cycling. Petra doesn't prefer cycling. Junho prefers swimming. Who prefers cycling?
Three friends, Ines, Cormac, and Wren, each ordered a different dessert: cake, pie, tart. Ines didn't order the tart. Cormac ordered pie. Who ordered the tart?
Three musicians, Soren, Amara, and Dax, each play a different instrument: guitar, drums, bass. Soren doesn't play bass. Amara plays drums. Who plays bass?
Three friends, Yara, Bram, and Isla, each drive a different vehicle: sedan, truck, van. Yara doesn't drive the van. Bram drives the truck. Who drives the van?
Three friends, Milo, Reza, and Ffion, each play a different sport: soccer, baseball, hockey. Milo doesn't play hockey. Reza plays baseball. Who plays hockey?

Four friends, Petra, Doran, Liv, and Amos, each ordered a different dish: pasta, salad, soup, sandwich. Petra didn't order pasta or the sandwich. Doran ordered salad. Liv didn't order the sandwich. Who ordered the sandwich?

Four students, Enzo, Ruth, Kwame, and Sena, each study a different subject: math, science, art, music. Enzo doesn't study art or music. Ruth studies science. Kwame doesn't study music. Who studies music?

Four friends, Odette, Hassan, Ines, and Bo, each have a different favorite season: spring, summer, autumn, winter. Odette's favorite isn't summer or winter. Hassan's favorite is autumn. Ines's favorite isn't winter. Whose favorite season is winter?

Four friends, Farah, Otis, Maren, and Cato, each have a different colored phone: blue, silver, black, white. Farah's phone isn't black or white. Otis's phone is silver. Maren's phone isn't white. Whose phone is white?

Four coworkers, Idris, Noelle, Petros, and Wyn, were each assigned a different region: north, south, east, west. Idris wasn't assigned east or west. Noelle was assigned south. Petros wasn't assigned west. Who was assigned west?

Four coworkers, Beatrix, Njeri, Osman, and Tamsin, each work a different shift day: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday. Beatrix doesn't work Wednesday or Thursday. Njeri works Tuesday. Osman doesn't work Thursday. Who works Thursday?

Four friends, Quinn, Radu, Selin, and Toby, each prefer different footwear: hiking boots, sandals, sneakers, slippers. Quinn doesn't prefer sneakers or slippers. Radu prefers sandals. Selin doesn't prefer slippers. Who prefers slippers?
Three friends, Amina, Luca, and Soraya, each ate a different staple food: bread, rice, pasta. Amina didn't eat pasta. Luca ate rice. Who ate pasta?

Three friends, Denis, Priya, and Oskar, each are reading a different kind of publication: novel, comic, magazine. Denis isn't reading the magazine. Priya is reading a comic. Who is reading the magazine?
Three artists, Mireille, Kenji, and Abel, each work in a different medium: painting, sketching, sculpture. Mireille doesn't work in sculpture. Kenji works in sketching. Who works in sculpture?
Three friends, Halle, Ronan, and Xia, each drink a different juice: apple, orange, grape. Halle doesn't drink grape juice. Ronan drinks orange juice. Who drinks grape juice?
Three friends, Tobias, Nadia, and Emre, each play a different sport: hockey, tennis, badminton. Tobias doesn't play badminton. Nadia plays tennis. Who plays badminton?
Three gardeners, Ingrid, Pavel, and Chidi, each grow a different flower: rose, tulip, daisy. Ingrid doesn't grow daisies. Pavel grows tulips. Who grows daisies?
Three friends, Saoirse, Dmitri, and Lina, each commute a different way: bus, train, bike. Saoirse doesn't commute by bike. Dmitri commutes by train. Who commutes by bike?

Four friends, Yusuf, Camille, Bashir, and Freya, each ordered a different drink: red wine, white wine, cider, beer. Yusuf didn't order cider or beer. Camille ordered white wine. Bashir didn't order beer. Who ordered beer?

Four coworkers, Arun, Zainab, Peder, and Mila, were each assigned a different office wing: north, south, east, west. Arun wasn't assigned east or west. Zainab was assigned south. Peder wasn't assigned west. Who was assigned the west wing?

Four musicians, Katarina, Boaz, Ifeoma, and Stellan, each play a different instrument: cello, harp, oboe, clarinet. Katarina doesn't play oboe or clarinet. Boaz plays harp. Ifeoma doesn't play clarinet. Who plays clarinet?

Four competitors, Rosalind, Achille, Ngozi, and Baptiste, each received a different award: gold medal, silver medal, bronze medal, ribbon. Rosalind didn't receive the bronze medal or the ribbon. Achille received the silver medal. Ngozi didn't receive the ribbon. Who received the ribbon?

Four developers, Esben, Ayaan, Marguerite, and Ochieng, each write in a different programming language: Python, Java, Rust, Go. Esben doesn't write Rust or Go. Ayaan writes Java. Marguerite doesn't write Go. Who writes Go?

Four coworkers, Perpetua, Njord, Aziza, and Colm, each work a different shift: morning, afternoon, evening, night. Perpetua doesn't work evening or night. Njord works afternoon. Aziza doesn't work night. Who works the night shift?

Four friends, Wilhelmina, Boyd, Nkechi, and Tobias, each chose a different ice cream flavor: strawberry, vanilla, chocolate, mint. Wilhelmina didn't choose chocolate or mint. Boyd chose vanilla. Nkechi didn't choose mint. Who chose mint?

This function should return the max of a list but has a bug:
def get_max(nums): return nums[0]
Find and fix it.

This function should sum all numbers from 1 to n inclusive but has a bug:
def sum_to_n(n):
    total = 0
    for i in range(1, n):
        total += i
    return total
Find and fix it.

This function should append to a fresh list each call but has a bug:
def add_item(item, items=[]):
    items.append(item)
    return items
Find and fix it so repeated calls don't share state.

This function should check if a number is even but has a bug:
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 1
Find and fix it.

This function should reverse a string but has a bug:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s
Find and fix it.

This function should return whether someone is an adult (18 or older) but has a bug:
def is_adult(age):
    return age > 18
Find and fix it.

This function should sum the digits of a number but has a bug:
def digit_sum(n):
    total = 0
    n = abs(n)
    while n > 0:
        total += n % 10
        n = n // 100
    return total
Find and fix it.

This function should return the running (cumulative) total before each number is added but has a bug:
def running_total(nums):
    result = []
    total = 0
    for n in nums:
        result.append(total)
        total += n
    return result
It should instead return the cumulative total after each number is added. Find and fix it.

This function should return whether a string is empty but has a bug:
def is_empty(s):
    return s == None
Find and fix it.

This function should return the product of all numbers in a list but has a bug:
def product(nums):
    result = 0
    for n in nums:
        result *= n
    return result
Find and fix it.

This function should compute the average of a list excluding one given value (only the first match, if it appears) but has a bug - it isn't excluding anything at all:
def average_excluding(nums, exclude):
    total = sum(nums)
    count = len(nums)
    return total / count
Find and fix it.

This function should compute n factorial iteratively but has a bug:
def factorial_iter(n):
    result = 1
    for i in range(1, n):
        result *= i
    return result
Find and fix it.

This function should check if n is a multiple of a given number but has a bug:
def is_multiple(n, of):
    return n % of == 1
Find and fix it.
Write a Python function called second_largest that returns the second-largest number in a list, handling duplicates correctly (e.g. [5, 5, 3] should return 3).
Write a Python function called is_palindrome that returns True if a given string reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring case, and False otherwise.
Write a Python function called flatten that takes a nested list of arbitrary depth and returns a single flat list of all the values, in order.
Write a Python function called count_vowels that returns the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) in a given string.
Write a Python function called nth_fibonacci that returns the n-th Fibonacci number (0-indexed, so nth_fibonacci(0) == 0 and nth_fibonacci(1) == 1).
Write a Python function called is_prime that returns True if a given positive integer is prime, and False otherwise.
Write a Python function called gcd that returns the greatest common divisor of two non-negative integers.
Write a Python function called remove_duplicates that returns a list with duplicate values removed, keeping only the first occurrence of each value in its original order.
Write a Python function called title_case that returns a copy of a string with the first letter of every word capitalized and the rest lowercase.
Write a Python function called sum_of_digits that returns the sum of the digits of the absolute value of a given integer.
Write a Python function called is_leap_year that returns True if a given year is a leap year (divisible by 4, except centuries which must be divisible by 400), and False otherwise.
Write a Python function called reverse_words that takes a sentence and returns it with the order of its words reversed, keeping each word itself unchanged.

Write a Python function called most_common_char that returns the character that appears most often in a string (you can assume there is always a single unique most-frequent character in the test inputs).
Write a Python function called is_perfect_square that returns True if a given non-negative integer is a perfect square, and False otherwise.
Write a Python function called rotate_list that rotates a list to the left by k positions (k may be larger than the list's length).
Write a Python function called word_count that returns the number of words in a string, treating any run of whitespace as a separator.

Write a Python function called is_armstrong_number that returns True if a given positive integer equals the sum of its own digits each raised to the power of the number of digits, and False otherwise.

Write a Python function called caesar_cipher_encode that shifts every letter in a string forward by a given number of positions in the alphabet, wrapping around from z to a, preserving each letter's case, and leaving non-letter characters unchanged.
Write a Python function called binary_to_decimal that converts a string of 0s and 1s into its decimal integer value.
Write a Python function called decimal_to_binary that converts a non-negative integer into its binary string representation, with no leading '0b' prefix.
Write a Python function called longest_word that returns the longest word in a sentence (you can assume there's always a single unique longest word in the test inputs).
Write a Python function called matrix_transpose that returns the transpose of a 2D list (a list of lists, all rows the same length).
Write a Python function called celsius_to_fahrenheit that converts a temperature in Celsius to Fahrenheit.
Write a Python function called is_pangram that returns True if a string contains every letter of the English alphabet at least once, ignoring case, and False otherwise.

args: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs

prompt: Write a Python function called chunk_list that splits a list into consecutive chunks of a given size, where the last chunk may be smaller than the rest.
Write a Python function called first_unique_char that returns the first character in a string that doesn't repeat anywhere else in it, or None if every character repeats.
Write a Python function called is_subsequence that returns True if every character of string a appears in string b in the same order (not necessarily contiguously), and False otherwise.
Write a Python function called max_subarray_sum that returns the largest possible sum of any contiguous subarray of a list of integers (the list always has at least one element).
